perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Approved Location. The street address of the location for the Franchised Business approved under this Agreement is specified in Exhibit A to this Agreement, and is referred to as the "Approved Location."
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Approved Location. The street address of the location for the Franchised Business approved under this Agreement will be determined at a later date and is not specified in Exhibit A, and is referred to as the "Approved Location."
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Approved Location' is modified to state that the location will be determined later and not specified in Exhibit A. This directly contradicts the original statement that the address is specified in Exhibit A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2.1 When this Agreement is signed, if you have not yet obtained (and we have not yet approved in writing) a location for the Franchised Business, then you agree to enter into the site selection addendum (the "Site Selection Addendum," attached as Exhibit G to this Agreement) at the same time as you sign this Agreement. You will then find a site which will become the Approved Location after we have given you our written approval for that site and you have obtained the right to occupy the premises, by lease, sublease, or acquisition of the property, all subject to our prior written approval and in accordance with the Site Selection Addendum.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Approved Location' to state that it will be determined later and is not in Exhibit A. This contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes that if a location hasn't been obtained at signing, the parties will enter a Site Selection Addendum (Exhibit G) to find a location that will 'become the Approved Location after we have given you our written approval for that site'. By stating the location is not in Exhibit A and will be determined later, it creates a direct conflict because the original text in 1.2.1 assumes a process that leads to an 'Approved Location', implying that Exhibit A should eventually reflect this. The modified text undermines this established process.

perturbation.original_text: 13.8 Our Review and Right to Approve All Proposed Marketing. For all proposed advertising, marketing, and promotional plans, you (or the Regional Fund, where applicable) must submit to us samples of such plans and materials (by means described in Section 24 below), for our review and prior written approval. If you (or the Regional Fund) have not received our written approval within fourteen (14) days after we have received those proposed samples or materials, then we will be deemed to have disapproved them. You acknowledge and agree that any and all copyright in and to advertising, marketing materials, and promotional plans developed by or on behalf of you will be our sole property, and you agree to sign such documents (and, if necessary, require your employees and independent contractors to sign such documents) that we deem reasonably necessary to give effect to this provision.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.8 Our Review and Right to Approve All Proposed Marketing. For all proposed advertising, marketing, and promotional plans, you (or the Regional Fund, where applicable) must submit to us samples of such plans and materials (by means described in Section 24 below), for our review and prior written approval. If you (or the Regional Fund) have not received our written approval within fourteen (14) days after we have received those proposed samples or materials, then you are automatically approved and can start the marketing. You acknowledge and agree that any and all copyright in and to advertising, marketing materials, and promotional plans developed by or on behalf of you will remain your sole property, and that we will not take ownership of those rights.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that a lack of response within 14 days means disapproval, and any copyrights are the company's. The modified clause contradicts this by stating that no response means automatic approval, and that all copyrights remain with the franchisee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.7 Standards. All of your local marketing and promotion must: (a) be in the media, and of the type and format, that we may approve; (b) be conducted in a dignified manner; and (c) conform to the standards and requirements that we may specify. You agree not to use any advertising, marketing materials, and/or promotional plans unless and until you have received our prior written approval, as specified in Section 13.9 below.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 13.8 directly contradicts both the original text of 13.8 and Section 13.7. The original 13.8 states that failure to approve within 14 days results in disapproval, while the changed text states it results in automatic approval. Section 13.7 states that marketing materials cannot be used until written approval is received, which is contradicted by the modified 13.8 as automatic approval after 14 days allows marketing to commence without written approval. Additionally, the change in copyright ownership in 13.8 from the company to the franchisee directly contradicts the original terms, further solidifying the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Initial Franchise Fee. You agree to pay us an initial franchise fee in the amount set out in the Declarations Page attached as Exhibit A (the "Initial Franchise Fee"). The Initial Franchise Fee is not refundable in consideration of administrative and other expenses that we incur in providing you with training, carrier appointments, and pre-opening assistance as part of the initial launch of the Franchised Business. At your election, the Initial Franchise Fee is due and payable to us in one of the following ways:
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Initial Franchise Fee. You agree to pay us an initial franchise fee in the amount set out in the Declarations Page attached as Exhibit A (the "Initial Franchise Fee"). The Initial Franchise Fee is fully refundable under any circumstances before the commencement date. At your election, the Initial Franchise Fee is due and payable to us in one of the following ways:
perturbation.explanation: Original Clause: The initial franchise fee is non-refundable. Perturbation: The initial franchise fee is fully refundable under any circumstances before the commencement date. The modified version directly contradicts the original statement that the Initial Franchise Fee is not refundable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Initial Franchise Fee. You agree to pay us an initial franchise fee in the amount set out in the Declarations Page attached as Exhibit A (the "Initial Franchise Fee"). The Initial Franchise Fee is fully refundable under any circumstances before the commencement date. At your election, the Initial Franchise Fee is due and payable to us in one of the following ways:
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the initial franchise fee is non-refundable. The changed text explicitly states that the initial franchise fee is fully refundable under any circumstances before the commencement date. This is a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. Since the 'Contradicted Text' is the same as the 'Changed Text', the contradiction exists with the original document. The change introduces an inconsistency that would cause confusion and potential legal disputes over the refundability of the fee.
